title: ActorKeyCache half-clear is O(n) scan while holding the write lock
description:
LOCATION: src/actor_cache.rs:92-99

FINDING: When the cache exceeds MAX_ENTRIES (10000), eviction calls entries.retain() (O(n) scan) and then, if still full, collects .keys().take(n/2).cloned().collect() into a HashSet and calls retain again — two O(n) passes while holding the Mutex. During eviction every concurrent inbox request that needs the cache also waits on this lock.

IMPACT: With 10000 entries, eviction scans ~80KB of key data twice while blocking all cache users. In practice, 10000 actors is a realistic ceiling for a well-followed instance. A more incremental eviction (LRU via a linked list or indexed-slab, or an async eviction task) would avoid the synchronous O(n) pause.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment on O(n) scan bounded by MAX_ENTRIES=10000
